title: Richly Spiced Vietnamese Tofu Bun Cha
description: A nutty and adventurous Vietnamese dish featuring ancho chillies and a cheese-pull effect, made with plant-based friendly ingredients, served in a bun cha form.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Start by rehydrating the ancho chillies in hot water for 20 minutes, then drain and chop them into small pieces.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, maple syrup, and sesame oil to make the marinade.
Add the cubed tofu to the marinade and let it sit for at least 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add the marinated tofu, cooking until golden brown on all sides.
In a small bowl, mix together minced garlic and grated ginger, then add it to the skillet with the tofu and cook for 1 minute.
Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
To assemble the dish, place some noodles in a bowl, followed by a few pieces of the tofu mixture, some pickled carrots, and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately and enjoy the combination of flavors and textures.
Garnish with additional cilantro and pickled carrots if desired.
Temper the dish by adding a small amount of hot water to the bowl and stirring gently.
